career_data.employment_history:
Senior Project Manager, Horizon Digital Solutions, Berlin, Germany (2019-01-01 – Present)
I lead a cross-functional team of product managers, UX designers, and software engineers to deliver B2B SaaS products for mid-sized logistics companies across the EU. I oversee the full project lifecycle from discovery and scoping to rollout and post-launch optimization. Key responsibilities include stakeholder management, budget oversight (projects ranging from €250k–€1.2M), risk management, and mentoring junior project managers. I introduced a standardized project intake process that reduced average kickoff time by 30%. I also co-created an internal “PM Guild” to share best practices and run monthly learning sessions.

Project Manager, UrbanTech Labs GmbH, Berlin, Germany (2014-03-01 – 2018-12-31)
Managed agile software development projects focused on smart city and mobility solutions. Worked closely with municipal clients in Germany and Austria to deliver mobile apps and data dashboards for public transport optimization. Coordinated sprint planning, backlog grooming, and release planning with distributed teams (Berlin and Vienna). Helped implement Scrum across the organization, including training product owners and facilitating retrospectives. Successfully led a 14-month project to launch a real-time bus tracking app used by over 200,000 daily commuters in Munich.

Business Analyst, Nordwind Consulting, Hamburg, Germany (2010-09-01 – 2014-02-28)
Worked as a business analyst for a boutique management consulting firm specializing in digital transformation for traditional manufacturing clients. Conducted requirements gathering workshops, process mapping, and cost-benefit analyses for ERP and CRM implementations. Developed functional specifications, test cases, and user training materials. Supported senior consultants in preparing client presentations and facilitated pilot rollouts at client sites.

Working Student (IT Support and Web Content), Hochschule für Angewandte Wissenschaften Hamburg, Hamburg, Germany (2008-10-01 – 2010-08-31)
Provided first-level IT support to staff and students, maintained departmental websites in a basic CMS, and assisted with small internal software rollouts. This role gave me early exposure to user support, documentation, and the importance of clear communication between technical and non-technical stakeholders.

Intern, Product Management, HanseCom Mobility Solutions, Hamburg, Germany (2008-03-01 – 2008-09-30)
Supported the product management team for a ticketing platform used by regional transportation providers. Assisted with market research, competitive analysis, and drafting product requirement documents. Participated in user testing sessions and helped summarize findings for the engineering team.
career_data.current_projects_activities:
1. Lead PM for “FreightFlow 2.0” Platform Migration (Horizon Digital Solutions)
I am currently leading a major migration of our core logistics management platform, “FreightFlow,” from a monolithic legacy architecture to a modular microservices-based system. The project involves coordinating three engineering squads (API, Frontend, Integrations), two external vendors, and key customers in Germany, Poland, and the Netherlands. My responsibilities include:
- Running discovery workshops with top logistics clients to prioritize features and ensure backward compatibility.
- Maintaining the master roadmap and aligning it with sales and customer success teams so they can communicate realistic timelines.
- Implementing a risk register and weekly risk review that has already helped us catch a potential integration delay with a customs API three months in advance.
- Facilitating quarterly big-room planning sessions, which we now run in a hybrid format (on-site Berlin + remote participants).

2. Internal Mentoring Circle for New Project Managers
Within Horizon Digital Solutions, I co-founded an informal mentoring circle for new or transitioning project managers (typically with under 2 years of experience). I meet with a group of 5 mentees once a month for 90 minutes. We:
- Review real project scenarios they are facing (e.g., handling a difficult stakeholder, estimating a complex feature set) and role-play conversations.
- Share practical templates (risk logs, communication plans, status report formats) and adapt them to their projects.
- Track individual development goals, such as improving facilitation skills or becoming more confident in saying no to unrealistic timelines.
I also have two 1:1 mentees with whom I meet every 4–6 weeks to go deeper on their personal career goals.

3. Ongoing Professional Development
I am currently preparing for the PMI-ACP (Agile Certified Practitioner) exam, aiming to sit for it in 2025-03. I am also enrolled in a 10-week online course on “Coaching Skills for Leaders” offered through an executive education platform. As part of this course, I keep a reflective journal of mentoring conversations at work, focusing on asking better questions instead of jumping directly to advice.

4. Volunteering: Tech Career Mentoring for Refugees
I volunteer with a Berlin-based NGO that supports refugees transitioning into tech careers. I currently mentor a software engineering student from Syria, meeting twice a month. We work on:
- Reviewing his CV and LinkedIn profile.
- Practicing mock interviews in English and German.
- Mapping out a realistic job search strategy for internships and junior roles in Berlin and Hamburg.
This experience has strongly influenced my interest in more structured mentoring programs.

5. Cross-Department Initiative: Knowledge Management Improvements
I’m part of a small internal working group tasked with improving how we document and share project learnings. We are piloting a new project retrospective format and a simple internal “playbook” site where PMs can document patterns and anti-patterns they observe in projects. My role is to design and facilitate the initial workshops and gather feedback from early adopters.
career_data.which_network: Berlin Product & Project Leaders Meetup; Women in Tech Germany; PMI Germany Chapter
career_data.which_mentoring_program: Women in Tech Germany Peer Mentoring Program (2020–2021 cohort); Internal Horizon Digital “Buddy & Mentoring” Program for New Managers (since 2021); NGO-led Tech Career Mentorship for Refugees (since 2022)
